summary: Full regression after backend rewrite (motor->pymongo sync, in-memory tokens->JWT, new wsgi.py via a2wsgi). 20/20 pytest backend assertions pass against public preview URL. All admin frontend flows verified end-to-end: JWT login (token saved as 'pal_admin_token', 3-segment JWT), all 5 admin tabs load, status dropdown change persists on bookings row, topper add+delete via UI, CSV export downloads pal_leads_export.csv, logout clears token and returns to login screen. Public demo form: consent unchecked shows exact error string, with consent shows success modal. No _id leaks anywhere. WSGI path NOT tested per instructions.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: JWT has no 'exp' claim — tokens are valid forever until JWT_SECRET rotates. For an admin panel with ADMIN_PASSWORD-only auth this is acceptable for MVP, but consider adding exp (e.g., 24h) plus iss/aud claims for production hardening.

server.py: JWT_SECRET defaults to 'pal-institute-default-secret-change-in-production' — must be overridden in PythonAnywhere env, otherwise anyone who reads the repo can mint admin tokens. Recommend failing fast (raise) when running in prod and JWT_SECRET equals the default.
server.py: admin_login has no rate limiting — brute-force protection should be added if exposed publicly (e.g., slowapi or Redis-based throttle).

server.py /admin/dashboard parse_dt() falls back to 'now' on parse failure, which inflates 'this week / this month' counts. Use a sentinel like datetime.min instead so unparseable rows are excluded from time-window buckets.

wsgi.py: a2wsgi.ASGIMiddleware spins up an asyncio loop per request. Because all routes are sync def + pymongo this is fine, but starlette CORS middleware still runs as ASGI middleware — verified via tests it works, but worth keeping in mind if any future async def or BackgroundTask is added.

server.py: pymongo MongoClient is module-level with serverSelectionTimeoutMS=10000 — under PythonAnywhere where workers fork, ensure MongoClient is re-created post-fork (a2wsgi doesn't fork, but if gunicorn preload_app is used elsewhere this can cause hangs).
server.py: CSV export and dashboard load up to 5000 docs into memory; fine for MVP but consider streaming/aggregation at higher volumes.
All endpoints correctly project {'_id': 0} — verified no leaks across all admin lists, public toppers, and create endpoints (which pop _id after insert).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added /api/health test, JWT format check, invalid-token verify test)
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_-prefixed bookings/enquiries/scholarship/topper records during pytest + UI runs. Persist in MongoDB pal_institute DB; cleanable via admin UI.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (20 tests). Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<preview_url> pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. Topper form testids are 'topper-name', 'topper-exam', 'topper-rank', 'topper-year' (no '-input' suffix), add btn 'topper-add-btn', delete 'topper-delete-{id}'. Admin tabs: admin-tab-{overview|bookings|enquiries|scholarship|toppers}. JWT stored under localStorage key 'pal_admin_token'. WSGI/PythonAnywhere path is NOT tested in this env — only ASGI/uvicorn.
rca of the issue: No regressions. Backend rewrite (motor->pymongo, in-memory->JWT) is functionally equivalent to iteration_1; all 20 backend tests + all 6 admin UI flows + public demo form pass.
